---
title: "EV market Analysis Dashboard"
format:
  dashboard:
    orientation: columns
    theme: cosmo
jupyter: python3
author: Sanodya Jinadasa
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from IPython.display import Markdown

df = pd.read_csv("ev_market_2026.csv")

```



# Overview

## column

```{python}
#| title: "Data Snapshot"
# Standard output for dataframes works fine without Markdown()
df.head(5)
```


## column

### Row
```{python}
#| echo: false
total_ev = df.shape[0]

f"""
<div style="
    background:#e3f2fd;
    padding:15px;
    border-radius:10px;
    text-align:center;">
    <h4>Total Vehicles</h4>
    <h2>{total_ev}</h2>
</div>
"""
```

### Row
```{python}
#| echo: false
total_ev = df.shape[0]

f"""
<div style="
    background:#e3f2fd;
    padding:15px;
    border-radius:10px;
    text-align:center;">
    <h4>Total Vehicles</h4>
    <h2>{total_ev}</h2>
</div>
"""
```


### Row
```{python}
#| echo: false
total_ev = df.shape[0]

# Markdown() tells Quarto to render the string as HTML
Markdown(f"""
<div style="background:#e3f2fd; padding:15px; border-radius:10px; text-align:center;">
    <h4 style="margin:0;">Total Vehicles</h4>
    <h2 style="margin:5px 0 0 0;">{total_ev}</h2>
</div>
""")
```



# Market & Brand Insights

# Performance vs Price

# Customer & Value Analysis

# Trends
